name: TACTIVE-U: AN INTERVENTIONAL SAFETY AND EFFICACY PHASE 1B/2, OPEN-LABEL UMBRELLA STUDY TO INVESTIGATE TOLERABILITY, PK, AND ANTITUMOR ACTIVITY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327), AN ORAL PROTEOLYSIS TARGETING CHIMERA, IN COMBINATION WITH OTHER ANTICANCER TREATMENTS IN PARTICIPANTS AGED 18 AND OLDER WITH ER+ ADVANCED OR METASTATIC BREAST CANCER, SUB-STUDY C (ARV-471 IN COMBINATION WITH SAMURACICLIB)

titles.text: TACTIVE-U: AN INTERVENTIONAL SAFETY AND EFFICACY PHASE 1B/2, OPEN-LABEL UMBRELLA STUDY TO INVESTIGATE TOLERABILITY, PK, AND ANTITUMOR ACTIVITY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327), AN ORAL PROTEOLYSIS TARGETING CHIMERA, IN COMBINATION WITH OTHER ANTICANCER TREATMENTS IN PARTICIPANTS AGED 18 AND OLDER WITH ER+ ADVANCED OR METASTATIC BREAST CANCER, SUB-STUDY C (ARV-471 IN COMBINATION WITH SAMURACICLIB)

TACTIVE-U: An Umbrella Study to Investigate the Safety and Antitumor Activity of vepdegestrant (ARV-471) in Combination with Other Medicines in Adults With ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer, Sub-study C (ARV-471 and Samuraciclib)

amendments.summary: The main purpose of the amendment is to point out the Phase 1b enrolment has been permanently closed due to a Sponsor decision and not for safety, efficacy reasons or requests from Agencies. No additional participants will be enrolled in Phase 1b and both Phase 2 and the DDI parts of the study will not be conducted. This amendment also introduces a reduced SoA allowing most clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician. In addition, this amendment is to update the end of study definition to allow treatment to continue for participants still receiving a benefit (defined as complete/partial response or stable disease by RECIST v1.1) from the study intervention.

primaryReason.otherReason: The main purpose of the amendment is to point out the Phase 1b enrolment has been permanently closed due to a Sponsor decision and not for safety, efficacy reasons or requests from Agencies. No additional participants will be enrolled in Phase 1b and both Phase 2 and the DDI parts of the study will not be conducted. This amendment also introduces a reduced SoA allowing most clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician. In addition, this amendment is to update the end of study definition to allow treatment to continue for participants still receiving a benefit (defined as complete/partial response or stable disease by RECIST v1.1) from the study intervention.

versions.rationale: To investigate the tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and antitumor activity of vepdegestrant (ARV-471) in combination with other anticancer treatments (sub-study C: ARV-471 + samuraciclib) in adults with ER+ advanced or metastatic breast cancer.

population.name: Main Population
population.label: Participants must be patients with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease including 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen. The current A/MBC disease must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST v1.1.
population.description: Participants must be patients with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease including 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen. The current A/MBC disease must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST v1.1.

arms.name: ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib
arms.label: ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib
arms.description:
Study intervention refers to ARV-471 and samuraciclib.

The starting dose level of ARV-471 will be 100 mg QD, administered continuously on 28-day cycles and with food. The starting dose of samuraciclib will be 240 mg QD administered continuously on 28-day cycles with or without food.

studyDesigns.name: Open-label Multicenter Phase 1b/2 Sub-study (ARV-471 + samuraciclib)
studyDesigns.label: C4891024 (sub-study C) is a prospective, open-label, multicenter, Phase 1b/2 sub-study to evaluate the safety, tolerability, antitumor activity, and PK of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib in the treatment of participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC.

epochs.name: Screening
epochs.label: Screening Period
epochs.description: The screening period will occur within 28 days prior to first dose.

epochs.name: Combination Treatment Period
epochs.label: Combination Treatment Period 1 Cycle = 28 days
epochs.description:
Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles.
Samuraciclib will be administered alone on Day -13 at the dose selected as RDE.
No treatment period on Day -12 and Day -11 (two days).
ARV-471 QD will be administered alone from Day -10 to Day -4, inclusive at the dose selected as RDE.
Samuraciclib will be administered in combination with ARV-471 on Day -3, followed by no treatment period for samuraciclib on Day -2 and Day -1; ARV-471 QD will continue to be administered alone during the no treatment period on Day -2 and Day -1.
ARV-471 QD will then be administered in combination with samuraciclib QD starting on C1D1.

epochs.name: Post-Treatment FollowUp
epochs.label: Post-Treatment FollowUp
epochs.description: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA.

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: Informed Consent Process
elements.description:
The investigator or qualified designee will explain the nature of the study, including the risks and benefits, to the participant and answer all questions regarding the study. The investigator may not delegate obtaining consent to a local healthcare professional.
The participant should be given sufficient time and opportunity to ask questions and to decide whether or not to participate in the trial.
Participants must be informed that their participation is voluntary. Participants (if allowed by local regulations) will be required to sign a statement of informed consent that meets the requirements of 21 CFR 50, local regulations, ICH GCP guidelines, privacy and data protection requirements, where applicable, and the IRB/EC or study center.
The medical record must include a statement that written informed consent was obtained before the participant was enrolled in the study and the date on which the written consent was obtained.

transitionEndRule.name: EndInformedConsent
transitionEndRule.text: written informed consent was obtained before the participant was enrolled in the study and the date on which the written consent was obtained

transitionStartRule.name: StartInformedConsent
transitionStartRule.text: Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and prior to any protocol-required assessments being performed

elements.name: EL3
elements.label: Samuraciclib single dose
elements.description: Samuraciclib will be administered alone on Day -13 at the dose selected as RDE.

transitionEndRule.name: EndSamuraciclibSingleDosePK
transitionEndRule.text: D-13: PK pre-dose; 1, 2, 4, 6, 8 hrs post dose (samuraciclib)

transitionStartRule.name: StartSamuraciclibSingleDose
transitionStartRule.text: Samuraciclib will be administered alone on Day -13 at the dose selected as RDE.

elements.name: EL4
elements.label: ARV-471 QD administered alone
elements.description: ARV-471 QD will be administered alone from Day -10 to Day -4, inclusive at the dose selected as RDE.

transitionStartRule.name: StartARV471Alone
transitionStartRule.text: ARV-471 QD will be administered alone from Day -10 to Day -4, inclusive at the dose selected as RDE.

elements.name: EL5
elements.label: Samuraciclib administered in combination with ARV-471 on Day -3
elements.description: Samuraciclib will be administered in combination with ARV-471 on Day -3, followed by no treatment period for samuraciclib on Day -2 and Day -1; ARV-471 QD will continue to be administered alone during the no treatment period on Day -2 and Day -1.

transitionStartRule.name: StartComboDayMinus3
transitionStartRule.text: Samuraciclib will be administered in combination with ARV-471 on Day -3

elements.name: EL6
elements.label: ARV-471 + Samuraciclib combination dosing (C1D1 and ongoing)
elements.description: ARV-471 QD will then be administered in combination with samuraciclib QD starting on C1D1.

transitionStartRule.name: StartCombinationC1D1
transitionStartRule.text: ARV-471 QD will then be administered in combination with samuraciclib QD starting on C1D1.

elements.name: EL7
elements.label: DDI Assessment Cohort(s)
elements.description: Depending on the doses selected for RDE(s), additional DDI Assessment Cohort(s) may be required to fully assess potential DDI at different dose levels. The participants from the DDI Assessment Cohort(s) will not be included in the DLT-evaluable population.

transitionEndRule.name: EndDDICohortPK
transitionEndRule.text: completion of the last PK sampling on Cycle 2 Day 1

transitionStartRule.name: StartDDICohort
transitionStartRule.text: Depending on the doses selected for RDE(s), additional DDI Assessment Cohort(s) may be required to fully assess potential DDI at different dose levels.

elements.name: EL8
elements.label: Follow-Up of AEs and SAEs
elements.description: After the initial AE or SAE report, the investigator is required to proactively follow each participant at subsequent visits/contacts. For each event, the investigator must pursue and obtain adequate information until resolution, stabilization, the event is otherwise explained, or the participant is lost to follow-up (as defined in Section 7.3).

transitionEndRule.name: EndAEFollowUp
transitionEndRule.text: until resolution, stabilization, the event is otherwise explained, or the participant is lost to follow-up

transitionStartRule.name: StartAEFollowUp
transitionStartRule.text: After the initial AE or SAE report, the investigator is required to proactively follow each participant at subsequent visits/contacts.

elements.name: EL9
elements.label: Post-Treatment Follow-up
elements.description: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA. For Phase 2 as reported in the SoA, survival status will be collected in the CRF every 3 months (±7 days) from the last dose of study treatment, including subsequent anticancer therapies (eg, start and stop dates, BOR and date of PD of the subsequent therapy), until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first.

transitionEndRule.name: EndPostTreatmentFollowUp
transitionEndRule.text: until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first.

transitionStartRule.name: StartPostTreatmentFollowUp
transitionStartRule.text: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA.

estimands.name: Primary Estimand
estimands.description: Primary Estimand : DLT rate estimated based on data from DLT-evaluable participants during the DLT observation period (Cycle 1). The estimand has the following attributes: Population: DLT-evaluable participants with A/MBC, defined by the inclusion and exclusion criteria to reflect the targeted population of the treatment, who receive at least 1 dose of the study interventions in Phase 1b and either experience DLT(s) during the DLT observation period or complete the DLT observation period without DLT. Participants without DLTs who receive less than 75% of the planned dose of each study drug during DLT observation period for reasons other than treatment-related toxicity are not evaluable for DLT.
estimands.populationSummary: DLT-evaluable participants with A/MBC who receive ≥1 dose of study interventions and either experience a DLT during the Cycle 1 DLT observation period or complete the DLT observation period; participants who receive <75% of planned dose for reasons other than treatment-related toxicity are excluded from the DLT-evaluable population.

intercurrentEvents.name: Insufficient dosing (<75% planned dose for non-treatment-related reasons)
intercurrentEvents.text: Participants without DLTs who receive less than 75% of the planned dose of each study drug during DLT observation period for reasons other than treatment-related toxicity are not evaluable for DLT.

studyDesigns.rationale: TACTIVE-U is an umbrella study comprising multiple sub-studies that independently evaluate ARV-471 in participants with ER+ A/MBC. ARV-471 will act as the backbone therapy given in combination with other anticancer agents thought to have clinical relevance in ER+ breast cancer.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01
objectives.text: Assess the safety and tolerability of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib in participants with ER+/HER2- advanced/metastatic breast cancer (A/MBC) to select up to two recommended dose levels for the combination.

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01_DLT
endpoints.text: Occurrence of dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) during the Cycle 1 DLT observation period in participants treated with ARV-471 plus samuraciclib.
endpoints.description:
DLTs during DLT observation period (Cycle 1).
The primary endpoint in Phase 1b is DLT during the DLT-observation period.

objectives.description: To assess safety and tolerability of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib in participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC to select up to 2 RDEs for the combination.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_01
objectives.text: Evaluate the overall safety profile of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib in participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_01_AEincidence
endpoints.text: Incidence and counts of adverse events (AEs) and serious adverse events (SAEs) observed during the study.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_02_LabAbnorm
endpoints.text: Incidence of clinically significant laboratory abnormalities during treatment with ARV-471 plus samuraciclib.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_03_ECGAbnorm
endpoints.text: Incidence of electrocardiogram (ECG) abnormalities observed during the study.

objectives.description: To evaluate the overall safety profile.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_02_EFFICACY
objectives.text: Evaluate the antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib in participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_04_ORR
endpoints.text: Confirmed objective response rate (ORR: confirmed CR or PR) by investigator assessment per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.description: Confirmed OR (CR or PR) by investigator assessment.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_05_DoR
endpoints.text: Duration of response (DoR) measured from first documentation of objective response to progression or death, by investigator assessment.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_06_CBR
endpoints.text: Clinical benefit rate (CBR) defined as confirmed CR or PR at any time, or stable disease (SD) lasting ≥24 weeks, by investigator assessment.
endpoints.description: CBR (confirmed CR or PR at any time, or SD ≥24 weeks) by investigator assessment.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_07_PFS
endpoints.text: Progression-free survival (PFS) defined as time from first dose to first documentation of disease progression or death from any cause, by investigator assessment.

objectives.description: To evaluate antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_03_PK
objectives.text: Evaluate the plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473, and samuraciclib when administered in combination.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_08_PKconc
endpoints.text: Plasma concentrations of ARV-471, ARV-473, and samuraciclib measured over specified PK sampling times.
endpoints.description: Plasma concentrations of ARV-471, ARV-473, and samuraciclib.

objectives.description: To evaluate the plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473, and samuraciclib when ARV-471 and samuraciclib are given in combination.

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_01
objectives.text: Explore tumor biology and the relationship between tumor mutational status and treatment response to ARV-471 plus samuraciclib.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_01_Biomarkers
endpoints.text: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, or defined cell types) from peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue biospecimens and descriptive analyses of their association with antitumor activity.
endpoints.description: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, or defined cell types) resulting from analyses of peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue biospecimen.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_02_GenomicsResponse
endpoints.text: Associations between tumor gene/genomic alterations and participant treatment responses and disease biology.
endpoints.description: Associations of tumor gene/genomic alterations with participant treatment responses and disease biology.

objectives.description:
To evaluate tumor biology and explore the treatment effect of ARV-471.
To evaluate relationships between mutational status and response in molecularly- defined participant population.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_02_Ph2_ORR
objectives.text: Assess the clinical antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib in Phase 2 as measured by investigator-assessed confirmed objective response rate (ORR).

endpoints.name: EP_PR_02_Ph2_ORR
endpoints.text: Confirmed objective response (CR or PR) determined by investigator assessment per RECIST v1.1 in the Phase 2 population.
endpoints.description: Confirmed OR (CR or PR) determined by investigator assessment.

objectives.description: To assess the clinical antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with samuraciclib.

scheduleTimelines.entryCondition: Allocation to study intervention must be completed after eligibility has been confirmed.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Other Inclusion criteria:

ECOG PS ≤1, no deterioration over the previous 2 week
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Other Inclusion criteria:

ECOG PS ≤1, no deterioration over the previous 2 weeks.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complications in
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complications in the short term, including participants with massive uncontrolled effusions (pleural, pericardial, and/or peritoneal), pulmonary lymphangitis, and liver involvement  50%.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with active bleeding, history of hemolytic anemia or marrow
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with active bleeding, history of hemolytic anemia or marrow aplasia.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST v1.1.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST v1.1. Participants with bone lesions only can be included if at least 1 bone lesion has a measurable component as for RECIST v1.1 (see Appendix 11).

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Phase 1b and DDI Assessment Cohort(s):

 Up to 2 lines of prior therapi
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Phase 1b and DDI Assessment Cohort(s):

 Up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease:

 ≤1 prior chemotherapy (no antibody-drug conjugates permitted).

 ≤2 prior endocrine-based therapies.

 No prior experimental endocrine therapy (eg, SERD, CERAN, PROTAC, SERCA) or elacestrant permitted.

 1, and only 1, line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen required (independent of the setting eg, adjuvant or advanced/metastatic).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleed
eligibilityCriteria.text: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleeding, active inflammatory bowel disease, inability to swallow the formulated product, or previous, significant gastric-bowel resection that would preclude adequate absorption of study interventions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic CNS metas
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic CNS metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease as indicated by clinical symptoms, cerebral edema, and/or progressive growth. Participants with a history of CNS metastases or cord compression are eligible if they have been definitively treated (eg, radiotherapy, stereotactic surgery) and clinically stable (including participants with residual CNS symptoms/deficits) and discontinued anti-seizure medications and corticosteroids for at least 28 days prior to first dose of IMP.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
11. Prior treatment/therapy with any of the following:

 Systemic antibiotic, a
eligibilityCriteria.text:
11. Prior treatment/therapy with any of the following:

 Systemic antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral medication within 14 days prior to allocation to study therapy.

 Systemic corticosteroids (at a dose >10 mg prednisone/day or equivalent) within 14 days before the first study dose, or within 28 days before the first study dose when administered to participants with a history of CNS metastases or cord compression (see Exclusion Criterion #3).

 A live-virus vaccination within 28 days or less of planned treatment start. Note : seasonal flu vaccines that do not contain live virus are permitted.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Renal impairment defined by an eGFR  45 mL/min/*. In equivocal cases, a
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Renal impairment defined by an eGFR  45 mL/min/*. In equivocal cases, a 24-hour urine collection test can be used to estimate the creatinine clearance more accurately.

Use the 2021 CKD-EPI eGFR (serum creatinine only) equation [see Appendix 7] to calculate eGFR (in units of mL/min/1.73 m²). By multiplication of ([eGFR] × [nomogram-based participant body surface area (m 2 )) = estimated creatinine clearance (eCrCl) in mL/min.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
13. Hepatic dysfunction defined as:
 Total bilirubin  1.5 × ULN unless the p

Other Exclusions:

Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Other Exclusions:

Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and sponsor and sponsor delegate employees directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in acco
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in accordance with local regulations) at screening.

Refer to Appendix 4 for reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.4) participants.

Male Participant Reproductive Inclusion Criteria

Male participants are eligible to participate if they agree to the following requirements during the intervention period and for at least 30 days after the last dose of ARV-471 and 4 months after the last dose of samuraciclib; which corresponds to the time needed to eliminate reproductive safety risk of the study intervention(s):

 Refrain from donating sperm.

PLUS either:

 Be abstinent from heterosexual intercourse as their preferred and usual lifestyle (abstinent on a long-term and persistent basis) and agree to remain abstinent.

OR

 Must agree to use contraception/barrier as detailed below:

 Agree to use a male condom and should also be advised of the benefit for a female partner to use a highly effective method of contraception as a condom may break or leak when having sexual intercourse with a IOCBP who is not currently pregnant.

Female Participant Reproductive Inclusion Criteria

The criteria below are part of Inclusion Criterion No. 1 (Age and Sex; Section 5.1) and specify the reproductive requirements for including female participants. Refer to Section 10.4.4 for a complete list of contraceptive methods permitted in the study.

A female participant is eligible to participate if they are (a) not pregnant or breastfeeding; (b) at least 1 of the following conditions applies:

 Is not a IOCBP (see definitions below in Section 10.4.3).

OR

 Is a IOCBP and agrees to use a contraceptive method that is highly effective (failure rate of <1% per year) with low user dependency during the intervention period and agree to use it for at least 4 weeks after the last dose of ARV-471 and 7 months after the last dose of samuraciclib, which corresponds to the time needed to eliminate any reproductive safety risk of the study intervention(s). The investigator should evaluate the effectiveness of the contraceptive method in relationship to the first dose of study intervention.

The investigator is responsible for review of medical history, menstrual history, and recent sexual activity to decrease the risk for inclusion of a woman with an early undetected pregnancy.

10.4.3. Woman of Childbearing and Non-Childbearing Potential

A woman is considered fertile following menarche and until becoming postmenopausal unless permanently sterile (see below).

If fertility is unclear (eg, amenorrhea in adolescents or athletes) and a menstrual cycle cannot be confirmed before the first dose of study intervention, additional evaluation should be considered.

Women in the following categories are not considered IOCBP:

Premenopausal female with 1 of the following:

 Documented hysterectomy;

 Documented bilateral salpingectomy;

 Documented bilateral oophorectomy.

For individuals with permanent infertility due to a medical cause other than the above, (eg, mullerian agenesis, androgen insensitivity), investigator discretion should be applied to determining study entry.

Note: Documentation for any of the above categories can come from the site personnel's review of the participant's medical records, medical examination, or medical history interview. The method of documentation should be recorded in the participant's medical record for the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Phase 2:

 Participants must have documented objective disease progression
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Phase 2:

 Participants must have documented objective disease progression while on or within 6 months after the end of the most recent therapy.

 Participants must have received an aromatase inhibitor in combination with a CDK4/6 inhibitor as the line of therapy preceding study entry for:

 Locally advanced or metastatic disease, participants must have received at least 6 months clinical benefit on this line of therapy to be eligible. Participants who received  6 months CDK4/6 inhibitor due to tolerability issues may still enter the study provided at least 6 months aromatase inhibitor was received.

OR

 Adjuvant treatment, if the disease-free interval between initiation of endocrine therapy and first line treatment of locally advanced or metastatic disease was >24 months.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
The following prior procedures and treatment are not permitted:

 Anti-cancer syste
eligibilityCriteria.text:
The following prior procedures and treatment are not permitted:

 Anti-cancer systemic therapies (Phase 2 only):

o More than 1 line of endocrine treatment for locally advanced or metastatic disease treatment.

o Prior therapy with selective SERDs, SERMs, SERCAs, CERANs, and PROTACs (approved or experimental), or elacestrant (estrogen receptor antagonist).

o Prior treatment with cytotoxic chemotherapy for locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer.

o Prior treatment with a mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR) inhibitor including, but not limited to, everolimus.

o Prior treatment with an PI3K inhibitor, including, but not limited to alpelisib.

 Major surgery, radiotherapy, prior endocrine therapy, CDK4/6 inhibitors, or other anticancer treatments within ≤28 days prior to first dose of IMP (for anticancer therapy containing an antibody-based agents, approved or investigational, 28 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is shorter).

 Participation in other clinical studies involving investigational drug(s) or vaccine(s) within 28 days or 5 half-lives prior to first dose of IMP (whichever is shorter). Participants may be eligible if they are in the follow-up phase of an investigational study provided that at least 5 half-lives have elapsed from the last dose.

 Participants who received: prior radiotherapy to ≥25% of bone marrow, or prior hematopoietic stem cell, or bone marrow transplantation are not eligible independent of when it was received (see Appendix 13).

Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies Grade >1 at the time of study enrollment except for Grade 2 alopecia, peripheral neuropathy, arthralgia, or other toxicities not considered a safety risk for the participant per the investigator's judgment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Histological or cytological diagnosis of breast cancer. At time of enrollmen
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Histological or cytological diagnosis of breast cancer. At time of enrollment this must not be amendable to surgical resection with curative intent (≥1% ER+ stained cells as per local practice [25] on the most recent tumor biopsy HER2- tumor by IHC or in-situ hybridization per ASCO/CAP) [26].

 Participants who have bilateral breast cancers that are both ER+/HER2- are eligible.

 Tumor block collected at the time of diagnosis with local recurrent or metastatic disease or archival tumor tissue is required for inclusion (Phase 1b and Phase 2). Refer to Section 8.7.

eligibilityCriteria.name: History of any other malignancies within the past three years, except for
eligibilityCriteria.text: History of any other malignancies within the past three years, except for the following: (1) adequately treated basal or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin; (2) curatively treated in situ carcinoma of the cervix. All other malignancies must have been curatively treated with no evidence of disease for  3 years. Participants with inflammatory breast cancer are excluded.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseas
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseases, as defined as:

 Any of the following in the previous 6 months: myocardial infarction, severe/unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class III or IV), cerebrovascular accident, transient ischemic attack, or symptomatic pulmonary embolism or other clinically significant episode of thromboembolic disease.

 Uncontrolled hypertension (defined as persistent systolic BP ≥160 mm Hg or diastolic BP ≥100 mmHg, despite optimal therapy). Symptomatic cardiac valve disease. Participants with mitral valve prolapse that is asymptomatic or not associated with clinically significant sequalae (eg, mitral regurgitation) are eligible.

 QTcF >470 msec (for women) or >450 msec (for men) at screening, or patients with risk factor of QT prolongation (eg, electrolytic abnormalities) unless mitigated or corrected prior to enrollment, or any of the following in the previous 6 months: congenital long QT syndrome, Torsade de Pointes, clinically important arrhythmias, left anterior hemiblock (bifascicular block), ongoing cardiac dysrhythmias of Grade ≥2, atrial fibrillation of any grade.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Prior/Concomitant Therapy:

Concurrent administration of medications, food, or
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Prior/Concomitant Therapy:

Concurrent administration of medications, food, or herb supplements that are strong inhibitors/inducers of CYP3A, strong CYP2D6 inhibitors and drugs known to predispose to Torsade de Pointes or QT interval prolongation. Prior use of strong CYP3A and CYP2D6 inhibitors must be stopped 7 days, strong inducers of CYP3A must be stopped 14 days before enrollment in the study. Refer to Sections 6.9.3 and Appendix 9 for the list of examples of prohibited drugs.

In addition, for participant to be enrolled in the DDI Assessment Cohort(s): Prior use of strong CYP2C19 inhibitors, and moderate CYP3A, CYP2C19 and CYP2D6 inhibitors must be stopped 7 days, moderate inducers of CYP3A, and strong and moderate inducers of CYP2C19 must be stopped 14 days before enrollment in the study. Refer to Sections 6.9.3 and Appendix 9 for the list of examples of prohibited drugs.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study or any social situations that would limit study compliance.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
14. Hematologic abnormalities defined as:
 ANC  1,500/mm 3 or 1.5 × 10 9 /L;
eligibilityCriteria.text:
14. Hematologic abnormalities defined as:
 ANC  1,500/mm 3 or 1.5 × 10 9 /L;

 Platelets  100,000/mm 3 or 100 × 10 9 /L;

 Hemoglobin  9 g/dL. Receipt of a blood transfusion (blood or blood products) within 14 days before the first dose.

Known active infection including hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, and HIV or AIDS-related illness (screening for chronic conditions is not required). A participant who has a positive test result for SARS-CoV-2 infection, is known to have asymptomatic infection, or is suspected of having SARS-CoV-2, is not eligible for the study, except in case SARS-CoV-2 infection occurring during the screening period is resolved at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

Note : active viral hepatitis infection is defined as untreated/uncontrolled hepatitis (eg, hepatitis B participants who are treated and whose LFTs meet eligibility are not considered to have active hepatitis B).

For HIV infection, the study participant is eligible if the disease is controlled with treatment, and the therapy does not exclude the participant as per Exclusion Criterion #10.

studyInterventions.name: Samuraciclib
studyInterventions.description: Investigational oral CDK7 inhibitor administered in combination with ARV-471 to evaluate safety and activity in ER+ advanced/metastatic breast cancer.

administrations.name: Samuraciclib 120 mg QD — Continuous 28-day cycles
administrations.description: One 120 mg capsule daily. May be taken with or without food; if nausea or vomiting occurs, take during or after a meal. If a dose is missed do not double-up.

administrations.name: Samuraciclib 240 mg QD — Continuous 28-day cycles
administrations.description: Two 120 mg capsules daily. May be taken with or without food; if nausea or vomiting occurs, take during or after a meal. If a dose is missed do not double-up.

administrations.name: Samuraciclib 360 mg QD — Continuous 28-day cycles
administrations.description: Three 120 mg capsules daily. May be taken with or without food; if nausea or vomiting occurs, take during or after a meal. If a dose is missed do not double-up.

administrableProducts.name: Samuraciclib
administrableProducts.description: 120 mg capsules provided in a high-density polyethylene bottle with child-resistant cap.

substance.name: samuraciclib
substance.description: ATP-competitive, selective oral CDK7 inhibitor that blocks CDK7 catalytic activity, inhibiting cell-cycle progression and transcriptional regulation.

studyInterventions.name: ARV-471
studyInterventions.description: Investigational PROTAC agent evaluated in combination therapy to degrade ER in participants with ER+ advanced/metastatic breast cancer.

administrations.name: ARV-471 100 mg QD — Continuous 28-day cycles
administrations.description: Take in the morning with food at approximately the same time each day. If a dose is missed do not double-up. If vomiting after dosing, do not re-dose that day.

administrations.name: ARV-471 200 mg QD — Continuous 28-day cycles
administrations.description: Two 100 mg tablets taken in the morning with food at approximately the same time each day. If a dose is missed do not double-up. If vomiting after dosing, do not re-dose that day.

administrableProducts.name: ARV-471
administrableProducts.description: 100 mg tablets provided in a high-density polyethylene bottle with child-resistant cap.

substance.name: ARV-471
substance.description: PROTAC small molecule that induces degradation of the estrogen receptor (ER) via recruitment of the cereblon E3 ligase and the ubiquitin-proteasome system.
